title: cyathea hortulanea
intro: Cyathea hortulanea is a type of fern species, belonging to the Cyathea genus, commonly found in Australia, New Zealand, and other parts of the Pacific Islands. It has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and even aid in the treatment of certain skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Cyathea hortulanea include its ability to promote skin health, reduce inflammation, and even aid in the healing process of wounds. In herbal preparations, Cyathea hortulanea is often used in topical creams, ointments, and salves, as well as in teas, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
